title: Erlang/OTP: Un Mundo Concurrente
author: Brujo Benavides
summary: Mi Reseña Luego de casi quince años de trabajar, enseñar y difundir Erlang por el mundo, recién hace unos pocos días tuve la posibilidad de leer mi primer libro sobre Erlang en castellano . Se trata del libro que da título a este artículo y está escrito por Manuel Rubio . Aquí van mis opiniones… Sí, el libro en papel…

title: Erlang’s Halloween
author: Brujo Benavides
summary: Some dead code that not even Dialyzer can find It’s relatively unusual to watch me talking about the shortcomings or limitations of such an excellent tool as Dialyzer . Still, today I found some very dead code blocks that slipped through its world-famous success type analysis. So, since Halloween is close, let’s raise some dead code from its grave! By…

title: Oh, Erlang!
author: Brujo Benavides
summary: What we did for #SpawnFest 2021 For almost ten years, I waited patiently but actively. And finally, the time has come! I was able to participate again in my all-time favorite hackathon: SpawnFest . In this article, I’ll show how Mariano, Manuel , Tetiana, and I spent the last 48 hours emojifying as many Erlang modules as possible. Yes, that’s…

title: Galicia ❤️ The BEAM
author: Brujo Benavides
summary: The impressive Galician contributions to the Erlang Ecosystem We all know about the most renowned Erlang/Elixir centers worldwide, like Sweden, Brazil, California, and London. But the community, even when not huge, is broadly spread. Today we want to celebrate a smaller region that’s home to many amazing contributions to our beloved ecosystem. The birthplace of my ancestors: Galicia! The other…

title: Spot the Difference
author: Brujo Benavides
summary: The erlang:process_info/1,2 version DISCLAIMER This article is not meant to be an instructive one. It’s written just for fun! 🙃 Erlang is famous for its introspecting powers. title: How to Comprehend Comprehensions
author: Brujo Benavides
summary: Particularly for Erlang Good Will Hunting (1997) So, I Gusti Ngurah Oka Prinarjaya was reading Joe’s Book and he found one of the most amazing examples of List Comprehensions I’ve ever seen… perms([]) -> [[]]; perms(List) -> [ [H|T] || H <- List, T <- perms(List--[H]) ]. Output: 1> lib_misc:perms("123"). ["123","132","213","231","312","321"] And, of course… he couldn’t understand it. And, as…
